understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to navigate selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+') in each level. Completing all levels means the whole game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based using pixel coordinates. MOVE actions shifted the currently selected piece in the given direction. Some objects (blocks) could be pushed, and walls blocked movement. The 'movement blocked' hints were not always accurate; relying on actual try-and-learn was required. Multiple selectable pieces existed, and switching control was done via CLICK on a piece's pixel position. The run ended positively after a series of successful MOVE_RIGHT actions, likely covering all goals on the last level.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to navigate selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+') in each level. Completing all levels means the whole game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based using pixel coordinates. MOVE actions shifted the currently selected piece in the given direction. Some objects (blocks) could be pushed, and walls blocked movement. The 'movement blocked' hints were not always accurate; relying on actual try-and-learn was required. Multiple selectable pieces existed, and switching control was done via CLICK on a piece's pixel position. The run ended positively after a series of successful MOVE_RIGHT actions, likely covering all goals on the last level.
